intake_prose:
We have paired primary colorectal cancer tumor and adjacent native mucosa biopsies from 73 patients, deposited under GEO accessions GSE156613 (147 H3K27ac + 86 H3K4me3 + 147 WGS-input ChIP-seq libraries) and GSE156614 (144 paired RNA-seq libraries). We want to recreate the Li et al. 2021 Nat Commun analysis end-to-end.

ChIP-seq processing: cutadapt v1.16 for adapter trimming, BWA v0.7.15 with default parameters against hg19 (paper does not specify aln vs MEM), MACS2 broad peak calling at p < 1e-10 with each ChIP sample paired against its matched WGS input lane. Patients 20, 21, 22, and 24 are excluded for low peak count or low VEL count.

Promoters are H3K4me3 peaks within 2.5 kb of a TSS; enhancers are H3K27ac peaks at least 2.5 kb from any TSS. Super enhancers are called by ROSE with 12.5 kb stitching and the tangent-of-curve cut.

Variant enhancer loci (VELs) are identified by paired t-test on H3K27ac signal between tumor and native, Benjamini-Hochberg correction (q < 0.1), and recurrence thresholds set so that 95% of called VELs achieve significance: recurrence >= 14 for gain VELs and >= 19 for lost VELs. Variant super-enhancer loci (VSELs) use the same framework with recurrence >= 10 and a relaxed 0.9 significant-percentage cut-off (smaller SE set). The headline integers we want to reproduce are 6,690 total VELs (5,590 gain + 1,100 lost) and 455 total VSELs (334 gain + 121 lost).

RNA-seq: cutadapt v1.16 -> HISAT2 v2.1.0 against hg19 with GENCODE v19 -> featureCounts v1.4.6 -> DESeq2 v1.26.0 with model ~ patient + condition. DEG threshold |log2FC|>1 and BH-padj<1e-5. Headline: 2,226 up / 1,979 down regulated genes.

Subtype stratification: CMSCaller v0.99.2 on RNA-seq -> CMS1/2/3/4. Per-subgroup VEL recurrence at the same significance cut-off. CMS2 should have the highest gain VEL count (~5,826).

Motif analysis: HOMER findMotifsGenome.pl on nucleosome-free regions of VELs (NFRs from PARE v0.08); we expect FOSL2 and JUNB at top, with ASCL2 emerging as the highest-degree TF from core-regulatory-circuit analysis with crc software (linlabbcm/CRC). GREAT v3.0.0 for disease ontology enrichment; ClueGO v3.8.0 for CMS2 pathway analysis.

Cross-dataset compare against Cohen 2017 (GSE77737) for novelty, HCT116 BRD4 (GSE126221) and H3K4me1 (ENCFF557VIT) for orthogonal validation, TCGA-COAD/READ for DE replication.
